references.name: TransformerLensOrg/TransformerLens
references.summary: Residual-stream hooks + ActivationCache library for decoder-only transformers; the primary mech-interp instrument.
references.relevance_notes: Used in every Phase 1 notebook via HookedTransformer + run_with_cache. Originally by Neel Nanda, now maintained by Bryce Meyer.

references.name: jbloomAus/SAELens
references.summary: Loader and analysis library for pre-trained sparse autoencoders (incl. Gemma Scope).
references.relevance_notes: Phase 2 entry point for SAE feature browsing; SAE.from_pretrained() pulls Gemma Scope weights.

references.name: safety-research/circuit-tracer
references.summary: Open-source attribution-graph engine released by Anthropic / Decode Research in 2025; produces input-specific causal graphs over interpretable features.
references.relevance_notes: Phase 3 entry point. Supports Gemma-2 (2B) and Llama-3.2 (1B). Backbone of Ameisen et al. 2025 and Lindsey et al. 2025 (Biology of a Large Language Model).

references.name: callummcdougall/ARENA_3.0
references.summary: ARENA mech-interp curriculum (Chapter 1 covers transformers from scratch, probes, SAEs, IOI, superposition).
references.relevance_notes: The structured curriculum this codebase shadow-follows. Phase 1 maps to ARENA Day 2-3; Phase 2 to the SAE chapter.

references.name: ndif-team/nnsight
references.summary: Hook + intervention library from David Bau's group (Northeastern NDIF) for activation patching and remote execution on production-scale models.
references.relevance_notes: Alternative to TransformerLens that scales past local memory. Backend option inside circuit-tracer. Worth comparing when Gemma-2-2B starts to feel small.

references.name: stanfordnlp/pyvene
references.summary: Causal-abstraction and distributed-alignment-search (DAS) intervention library from Stanford NLP.
references.relevance_notes: The reference toolkit for the causal-abstraction line of mech-interp work (Geiger et al. 2023). Natural companion to the RAVEL benchmark.

references.name: saprmarks/feature-circuits
references.summary: Implementation of Sparse Feature Circuits (Marks et al. 2024) — SAE features as nodes in an automatically discovered causal graph, with feature-level editing.
references.relevance_notes: Worked example of replacing neurons with SAE features as circuit nodes. Echoes the Phase 2 → Phase 3 transition this codebase is rehearsing.

references.name: callummcdougall/sae_vis
references.summary: Sparse autoencoder feature visualisation tool (top-activating prompts, logit attribution, distribution plots).
references.relevance_notes: Drop-in for the Phase 2 "one-paragraph human gloss per feature" task once Gemma Scope features are identified.

references.name: openai/sparse_autoencoder
references.summary: Companion code to Gao et al. (2024), "Scaling and Evaluating Sparse Autoencoders" — the top-k SAE that became the architectural default after mid-2024.
references.relevance_notes: Reference architecture for the top-k variant whose theoretical motivation the Phase 2 notebook will lean on. Useful for comparing against Gemma Scope's L1-style SAEs.
